repo_prior_art.cite: none; the Panel catalogue contains linear FWL/sign-flip stacks, not an effect-dependent PPML pseudo-true derivative rule.

published_prior_art.cite: MoreauKastler2025PTT §3.1/(3.6) gives a two-individual, three-period PPML wrong-comparison example, but not this general derivative formula; its FE specification differs once cohorts contain multiple units.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue PPML primitive sign-frontier proposal was found.

published_prior_art.cite: MoreauKastler2025PTT §3.1 stops at the two-individual, three-period illustration; GoodmanBacon2021 and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020TWFE concern linear-TWFE weights, not elimination of nonlinear PPML margins.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 / related_work / comparator_promise_table
novelty_flags.one_line: MoreauKastler2025PTT §3.1 specifies unit and time effects (alpha_i,beta_t), whereas the proposal's r_gt uses cohort and time effects; calling the multicohort result a strict tightening is unsupported beyond the one-unit-per-cohort special case.

soundness_flags.label: target_estimand, tldr, and Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The prose calls beta_star a unit-and-time-FE PPML coefficient, but the formal sample and population objectives include cohort-and-time fixed effects only.

soundness_flags.label: def:within-cohort potential-outcome mean
soundness_flags.one_line: barY_dgtN divides by n_gN although the declared range permits n_gN=0; require positive cohort counts for all sufficiently large N or define the sequence only there.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1
soundness_flags.one_line: {beta_star(delta)=0} need not equal the topological boundary of the strict sign region without a crossing condition; ask for the zero-level set or prove the relative-boundary assertion.

soundness_flags.label: bibliography: Andrews1988LLN
soundness_flags.one_line: The cited Andrews article is correctly identified by title, venue, year, and pages, but its DOI is 10.1017/S0266466600013396, not the listed ...3393.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Choose and state one estimand scope: either extend the analysis to unit-and-time-FE PPML with within-cohort baseline heterogeneity, or relabel the contribution throughout as a cohort-and-time-FE Poisson projection and downgrade Moreau-Kastler from a strict comparator to informed-by prior art.

Add an explicit corrected estimator or cohort-time imputation/aggregation procedure and compare its target with the contaminated pooled coefficient; this would turn the diagnostic into a diagnose-then-correct contribution.
Repair the finite-count domain, define the frontier as a zero-level set unless a crossing lemma is included, and correct the Andrews DOI.

recommended_next_step: Continue revision on this angle: align the formal PPML fixed-effect specification with its claimed comparator, then repair the two well-posedness details before advancing to Stage 0.
